_comment: Synthetic design-handoff briefs for C2 evaluation. 12 stratified briefs, matched 1:1 by id to labels in eval/design-handoff-labels.json. Every product name is fictitious and every URL uses example.com — no real product names or real domains appear here.

briefs.kind: Public marketing site for a fictional B2B SaaS brand.
briefs.goal: Convey the value proposition above the fold and route visitors to a single primary call to action.

sourceMaterial.note: Source hero pairs a headline left of a product screenshot.
Source nav uses a single top bar with a trailing CTA.

constraints: No third-party tracking on the landing route.
Primary CTA must be reachable without scrolling on a 1280px viewport.

briefs.kind: Analytics dashboard for a fictional operations team.
briefs.goal: Surface three KPIs and a recent-activity feed without overwhelming first-time users.

sourceMaterial.note: Source KPI cards use 16px gutters and a fixed 240px sidebar.
Source chart region reserves 320px of vertical space.

constraints: Sidebar must collapse on viewports under 1024px.
KPI cards must degrade to a single column on narrow viewports.

briefs.kind: Consumer money-movement surface for a fictional fintech.
briefs.goal: Let a user review and confirm a transfer with full clarity on amount, recipient, and timing.

sourceMaterial.note: Source amount field is right-aligned with a currency prefix.
Source confirm step surfaces a plain-language summary before submit.

constraints: Every monetary value must show its currency.
The confirm action must be visually distinct from a destructive one.

briefs.kind: Mobile-only capture flow for a fictional field-research app.
briefs.goal: Let a user capture a note in under three taps from a cold start.

sourceMaterial.note: Source capture entry is a floating action button within thumb reach.
Source form uses large tap targets and no horizontal scrolling.

constraints: Primary action must sit in the bottom thumb zone.
Inputs must remain usable in a single-handed portrait grip.

briefs.kind: First-run onboarding flow for a fictional planning tool.
briefs.goal: Collect three setup answers without losing the user before the first useful screen.

sourceMaterial.note: Source onboarding breaks setup into three single-purpose steps.
Source progress indicator reflects completed steps only.

constraints: Each step must have exactly one primary action.
Progress may not imply a step is complete before it is.

briefs.kind: Dense operations console for a fictional incident-response team.
briefs.goal: Let an operator triage a live incident from a single screen without losing context.

sourceMaterial.note: Source table packs 12 columns with sticky headers.
Source detail panel docks to the right and persists across row selection.

constraints: Density must not come at the cost of a 4.5:1 contrast floor.
Sticky headers must remain legible over long row sets.

briefs.kind: Early-concept surface with deliberately thin source evidence.
briefs.goal: Propose a defensible direction while being explicit about what is not yet grounded.

sourceMaterial.note: Only one sourced observation exists for this brief; the rest must be flagged as assumptions.

constraints: Every non-sourced direction must be recorded as an explicit assumption.
Source coverage is expected to be low.

briefs.kind: Surface where two source references disagree about a layout decision.
briefs.goal: Reconcile the conflict with a stated authority lane rather than silently picking one.

sourceMaterial.note: Reference A places the primary action at the top of the form.
Reference B places the primary action at the bottom of the form.

constraints: The chosen placement must be recorded as a retain/adapt/reject decision.
The rejected option must carry a rationale.

briefs.kind: Surface whose only archived reference is no longer reachable.
briefs.goal: Ground the direction in reachable evidence and explicitly mark the unreachable reference as not inspected.

sourceMaterial.note: A legacy archive URL exists but is no longer reachable.

constraints: The unreachable URL must not be claimed under inspectedUrls.
Direction must be supported by the reachable reference alone.

briefs.kind: Multi-page public site for a fictional sustainability nonprofit.
briefs.goal: Keep navigation, footer, and heading rhythm consistent across five top-level pages.

sourceMaterial.note: Source nav repeats across every page with the same item order.
Source footer is identical across pages.
Source pages use a shared H1/H2 rhythm.

constraints: Each top-level page must declare its own blueprint sharing the global direction.
Nav order must not drift between pages.

briefs.kind: Consent surface for a fictional productivity app.
briefs.goal: Obtain explicit consent while keeping 'reject all' as easy to reach as 'accept all'.

sourceMaterial.note: Source consent surface keeps accept and reject at the same visual weight.
Source surface separates strictly-necessary from optional categories.

constraints: 'Reject all' must be a single action.
Optional categories must default to off.

briefs.kind: Surface where the stakeholder explicitly asks to copy a named external reference.
briefs.goal: Honor the named-reference request only where it is safe and lawful to do so, and flag the rest as assumptions.

sourceMaterial.note: The named reference is a generic layout pattern (safe to adapt).
The named reference's wordmark and palette are proprietary (must not be copied).

constraints: Proprietary identifiers from the reference must not appear in the handoff.
The adaptation must be recorded under an adapt lane with a rationale.
